name: TikTok
description: The short-video platform — ByteDance, viral creators, algorithmic culture, and political controversy

questions.q: Which Chinese tech company owns TikTok?

source.notes: Also operates the Chinese version, Douyin.

questions.q: What is the name of TikTok's algorithm-driven main feed?

source.notes: Widely credited as the engine of TikTok's addictiveness.

questions.q: TikTok merged with which other short-video app in 2018?

source.notes: ByteDance bought Musical.ly for ~$1 billion in 2017.

questions.q: Which American dancer became the first TikTok account to surpass 100 million followers?

source.notes: Hit the milestone in November 2020.

questions.q: Which Senegalese-Italian creator, famous for wordless reaction videos, surpassed Charli D'Amelio as TikTok's most-followed user?

source.notes: Broke the record in mid-2022.

questions.q: In which country did ByteDance first launch Douyin, the domestic predecessor of TikTok?

source.notes: Launched 2016; TikTok launched internationally in 2017.

questions.q: Which Lil Nas X song became a huge TikTok viral hit in 2019 thanks to a cowboy-themed challenge?

source.notes: Record-breaking 19 weeks at No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100.

questions.q: Which collaboration feature lets users film alongside another user's video side-by-side?

source.notes: Stitch lets you incorporate a short clip at the start of your own video.

questions.q: Which country became the first major market to ban TikTok outright, in mid-2020?

source.notes: Banned along with 58 other Chinese apps in June 2020.

questions.q: Which TikTok user's viral skateboarding video set to Fleetwood Mac's 'Dreams' reignited interest in the song in 2020?

source.notes: Sent 'Dreams' back into the Billboard Top 40 in late 2020.

questions.q: Which 1985 Kate Bush song resurged globally after featuring on Stranger Things via TikTok in 2022?

source.original_a: Kate Bush's Running Up That Hill
source.notes: Topped charts worldwide, 37 years after release.

questions.q: What hashtag ecosystem revived interest in novels (notably romantasy) through TikTok?

source.notes: Helped propel Colleen Hoover and Rebecca Yarros up bestseller lists.

questions.q: Douyin and TikTok use nearly identical tech — but they are separate apps for what main reason?
choices: Douyin serves the Chinese domestic market only
Douyin is paid and TikTok is free
Douyin is owned by a rival

source.original_a: In which country did TikTok originate?
source.notes: Chinese regulations require a separate domestic app.

questions.q: Which early Twitter-owned short-video app was shut down in 2017, clearing the way for TikTok's rise?

source.notes: Limited videos to six-second loops.

questions.q: Which TikTok feature, launched in 2022, shares ad revenue with eligible creators on top videos?

source.notes: Targets the top 4% of videos on the platform.

questions.q: Which 2022 U.S. law banned TikTok on most federal government devices?
choices: No TikTok on Government Devices Act

source.original_a: No TikTok on Government Devices Act
source.notes: Signed into law at the end of December 2022.

questions.q: Who is TikTok's CEO as of the mid-2020s?

source.notes: Singaporean executive who took the role in March 2021.

questions.q: Which U.S. state was the first to pass (briefly) a full consumer ban of TikTok before courts blocked it?

source.notes: Signed by Governor Greg Gianforte in May 2023; blocked by a federal judge.

questions.q: In what year was TikTok first launched internationally as a standalone app outside China?

source.notes: Unified globally with the merger of Musical.ly in 2018.

questions.q: Who founded ByteDance, the parent company of TikTok?

source.notes: Founded ByteDance in 2012.

questions.q: TikTok was fined approximately how much by the FTC in 2019 for children's privacy violations — a U.S. record at the time?

source.notes: For COPPA violations, largest-ever FTC children's-privacy fine at the time.

questions.q: What is the name of the restricted Chinese version of TikTok offered by ByteDance inside China?

source.notes: Has stricter content rules and time-use limits for minors.
